cards.meaning: thus; thereby; consequently (formal connective linking a cause to its resulting effect)
cards.example: 资本支出激增，从而自由现金流转为负。
cards.examplePinyin: zī běn zhī chū jī zēng, cóng ér zì yóu xiàn jīn liú zhuǎn wéi fù
cards.exampleMeaning: Capital spending surged, thereby turning free cash flow negative.
cards.note: 书面语 / finance-desk register. Unlike 因此 (yīn cǐ, general 'therefore') or 于是 (yú shì, temporal 'and so'), 从而 marks a purposeful RESULT of the same subject's action — the earlier clause enables the later one. Pairs with a deliberate consequence, not a coincidence. Keep-it-handy chunk: X，从而 Y = 'X, and as a result Y.'
cards.tiesTo: Alphabet/Tesla capex surge turning free cash flow negative

cards.meaning: be bound to; will inevitably (expresses logical inevitability)
cards.example: 油价持续上涨，势必推高通胀预期。
cards.examplePinyin: yóu jià chí xù shàng zhǎng, shì bì tuī gāo tōng zhàng yù qī
cards.exampleMeaning: If oil keeps climbing, it is bound to push inflation expectations higher.
cards.note: 书面语 / formal, common in analyst notes. Stronger and more reasoned than colloquial 一定 (yí dìng, 'definitely') and different from 难免 (nán miǎn, 'hard to avoid') — 势必 asserts a logical, almost mechanical certainty from a stated premise. Collocation: often 势必 + verb of consequence (推高 / 导致 / 影响).
cards.tiesTo: Brent toward $98 → inflation and yields up into the Fed

cards.meaning: used up; depleted; completely spent, with nothing left
cards.example: Naubos ang cash ng kumpanya kahit tumaas ang benta.
cards.exampleMeaning: The company's cash got used up even though sales rose.
cards.note: Everyday register, but sharper than 'kulang' (short/insufficient) or 'salat' (scarce): 'ubos' means the well is DRY — total depletion, not a shortfall. Common forms: naubos (got used up), inubos (deliberately spent it all), paubos na (running out). The connotation is finality — you cannot draw on what is ubos.
cards.tiesTo: Negative free cash flow / the BSP spending dollar reserves

cards.meaning: a barrier; an obstacle that actively blocks progress or passage
cards.example: Ang kakulangan ng kuryente at tubig ang pinakamalaking hadlang sa pagbuo ng data center.
cards.exampleMeaning: The lack of power and water is the biggest barrier to building the data center.
cards.note: Register-neutral. Distinguish from near-synonyms: 'sagabal' is a nuisance in the way (something you step around), 'balakid' is a hurdle you clear — but 'hadlang' is a wall that genuinely stops you, and it collocates with abstract blockers (hadlang sa kaunlaran, hadlang sa kasunduan). Verb form: humadlang (to block), hinadlangan (was blocked).
cards.tiesTo: The power-and-water wall blocking the AI build / the court order blocking the Paramount–WBD deal
